settings.editor: Live test stand — every value below comes from an ACF repeater cell, addressed by repeater → row → sub-field through a native Elementor dynamic tag. Left: bound content. Right: what each block asserts.

arts-repeater-text / media / url on product_mockups rows 0–1. Each card is a fixed slot bound to one row: caption→title, description→blurb, image→image, link→button. Positional contract: reordering rows in wp-admin re-maps the cards. The italic line below binds row_index -1 — the “Last row” mode, always the newest item no matter how many rows exist.
arts-repeater-gallery · product_mockups row 0 → the Gallery control. One repeater cell feeds the whole grid, normalized to [{id,url}] — the GALLERY category shape.

The Video widget's YouTube URL is a meta-curated field (POST_META + URL categories) — arts-repeater-text qualifies because repeater cells are meta-backed; the video_youtube sub-field of row 0 drives the player. The second player is self-hosted: a File sub-field holding an mp4, bound via arts-repeater-media to “Choose Video File” (MEDIA category). The same cell also works on the External URL variant via arts-repeater-url.
arts-repeater-number · product_counters rows 0–1 value → Counter ending_number (NUMBER category, free-core widget). Labels come from the same rows via arts-repeater-text.

One rt_demo_items row feeds four categories at once: caption→TEXT (title), accent→COLOR (title color), count→NUMBER ($ counter), starts_at→DATETIME (Countdown — the display-formatted ACF value is re-parsed to Y-m-d H:i:s), cta→URL (button). The same starts_at cell ALSO renders as plain text below the countdown (“Sale ends …”) — there the output follows the ACF field's return format verbatim. Type bridges: the badge line is a select in 'array' return format rendered as its LABEL; the rich paragraph is a wysiwyg cell through wp_kses_post; “View related book” is a post_object cell resolved to its permalink by the URL tag.

Context ladder rung 0: rt_global_items lives on an ACF Options Page — no post data involved, values are site-wide. The address feeds Google Maps, a POST_META-only control. Editor row picker authorizes via the options page capability, not edit_post. Type bridges: “Studio:” extracts the google_map cell's address; “Perks:” joins a checkbox cell's labels with “, ”.

The heading is bound to row_index 99, which does not exist → the tag resolves to nothing (fail-closed) and Elementor's native Fallback renders instead. Before/After affixes wrap only non-empty values — so the “Offer:” prefix correctly disappears along with the missing row.
Visible on the frontend. Condition: rt_demo_items row count ≥ 2 — true, the repeater has 3 rows.
You should never see this on the frontend. Condition: rt_demo_items row count ≥ 99 — false. If this box renders, the condition failed.

arts-repeater-row-count (Pro Display Condition), evaluated per element on the frontend: the green box requires ≥2 rows (true — 3 rows), the red one ≥99 (hidden). The editor always shows both — conditions skip edit mode by design.

settings.title: 09 · Repeat mode — rows as loop items
One query result (this page) × 3 mockup rows → 3 cards above. Below: two rt_book posts × their own spec rows → 3 cards.

settings.editor: Repeat mode (“Repeat by ACF Repeater” on the Loop Grid's Query section): each queried post renders once per repeater row; card tags bind to Current loop row (row_index -2). Top grid: ONE post (this page) expands to 3 product cards. Bottom grid: two rt_book posts expand by their own row counts (2+1) — the “from:” line proves per-card post context. Pagination stays post-based by design; the sentinel outside a loop previews row 0.

sub_field values are depth-1 paths. The first heading reads meta.sku from rt_parent_items row 1 — the picker offers it as “Meta → SKU” in the same Sub-field dropdown; expect SKU-1001. The second heading binds a repeater living inside a top-level group (rt_nested_group.rt_group_repeater) — it joins the Repeater Field select like any other entry; bound to Last row, expect Inner row B. Plain sub-field names remain valid paths, so every pre-nested binding on this page is untouched.

Picking Specs (nested repeater) in the Sub-field select reveals a second picker pair: Nested Row + Nested Sub-field. Line 1: parent row 1 → nested row 2 → spec_name, expect Width. Line 2: parent row 1 → Last row → spec_value, expect 340 mm — follows the newest spec. Line 3 points at parent row 3, which has no specs (ACF returns false for an empty nested repeater): the tag fails closed and the Fallback “(no specs on this row)” renders. No “Current loop row” exists on the nested tier.

Flexible content joins the Repeater Field select; rows are addressed positionally and the picker labels each with its layout (“1 — Hero: …”). The Sub-field list is the union across layouts — heading appears once as “Hero, Quote → Heading” though both layouts define it. Line 1: row 1 (Hero) heading, expect Hero headline one. Line 2: row 2 (Quote) author, expect Jane Doe. Line 3 asks row 1 — a Hero — for author, which only the Quote layout has: the tag fails closed and the Fallback “(hero has no author)” renders instead of guessing.
